requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Supplier onboarding process undergoes internal approval workflow before sending invitation to supplier.
Supplier can register with AECI and complete due diligence steps after receiving invitation via email.
Supplier information is accurate and current, and certifications and contact information are visible.
Supplier creation and onboarding process is managed by Coupa SIM, and a supplier will only be created once they have completed the onboarding process.
Supplier status management includes purchase block, payment block, supplier deactivation, and reactivation.
Supplier data maintenance processes include external supplier updates via Coupa Supplier Portal and internal supplier updates initiated by AECI users.
Coupa SIM integrates with SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Internal users can update existing supplier information in Coupa, following the same approval workflow as new supplier registration.
Internal users can change the status of a supplier in Coupa, including blocking purchases or payments, deactivating, or reactivating the supplier.
Internal users can create a one-time vendor in Coupa with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements, while ensuring basic compliance and procurement controls.
Coupa displays different status labels to help users quickly see the status of each supplier, including Supplier Record Status and Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP) Status.
The system controls the visibility of inputs required from suppliers based on the country selected for the supplier, adhering to local guidelines and requirements.
Vendors are responsible for ensuring that all required documentation is accurate, complete, and up-to-date to facilitate timely review and approval.
Coupa supports the auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for internal users and suppliers, with the option to configure custom translations for specific geographies.
The system supports local language requirements.
